prompt: Calculate which phase (0-3) we are in. Phase is (elapsed / phase_duration) floored, then take modulo 4 to wrap around. Arguments: elapsed, phase_duration
Return true if elapsed >= target_time, false otherwise. Arguments: elapsed, target_time
Return opacity level (0 to 1) based on phase. Phases 0 and 2 (active breathing) return 1.0. Phases 1 and 3 (hold) return 0.5. Arguments: phase
Remap a value from one range to another. Given val in [in_min, in_max], map it to [out_min, out_max] linearly. Arguments: val, in_min, in_max, out_min, out_max
Linear interpolation between start and end values based on progress (0.0 to 1.0). Returns start when progress is 0, end when progress is 1, and linearly interpolates between them.
Add 1 to val and return the result. Arguments: val
Add 1 to p. If result >= 4, return 0. Otherwise return result. Arguments: p
Return 4000 if p is 0. Return 1000 if p is 1. Return 4000 if p is 2. Return 1000 if p is 3. Arguments: p
Add dt to t and return the result. Arguments: t, dt
Return true if t >= d. Otherwise return false. Arguments: t, d
If d is 0, return 0. Otherwise divide e by d and return result. Arguments: e, d
Clamp v to range [lo, hi]. If v is less than lo return lo. If v is greater than hi return hi. Otherwise return v. Arguments: v, lo, hi
Return true if val equals target, otherwise false. Arguments: val, target
If ph is 0 return 1 plus prog. If ph is 2 return 2 minus prog. Otherwise return 1. Arguments: prog, ph
Return true if idx is a valid index for an array of length len. Arguments: idx, len
Invert a boolean value. Arguments: val
Given a current position and a length, return the next position with wraparound. Arguments: pos, len
Clamp a value between a minimum and maximum. Arguments: val, min, max
Return true if elapsed time has reached or exceeded the threshold. Arguments: elapsed, threshold
Convert beats-per-minute to milliseconds per beat. Arguments: bpm
Accumulate elapsed time by adding delta time. Arguments: elapsed, dt
Get the phase of elapsed time within a beat cycle using modulo. Arguments: elapsed, beatLen
Increment val by 1, wrapping at max. If val + 1 >= max, return 0. Otherwise return val + 1. Arguments: val, max
Test equality. Return true if a equals b. Return false otherwise. Arguments: a, b
Check if bit is set. Return true if the bit at index idx is set in num. Use bitwise AND to check. Arguments: num, idx
Set a bit. Return num with the bit at index idx set to 1 using bitwise OR. Arguments: num, idx
Clear a bit. Return num with the bit at index idx set to 0. Arguments: num, idx
Clamp a value. If val is less than lo, return lo. If val is greater than hi, return hi. Otherwise return val. Arguments: val, lo, hi
Add two values. Return a plus b. Arguments: a, b
Toggle a bit. If the bit at index idx in num is 0, set it to 1. If it is 1, set it to 0. Return the result. Arguments: num, idx
